message:
fix(mvcc): handle epoch 0 correctly in EbrRetireQueue::drain_if_safe

The drain_if_safe() method used oldest_retire_epoch == 0 as a sentinel
to detect "no pending retirements". But epoch 0 is a valid retirement
epoch, so slots retired at epoch 0 were incorrectly treated as "empty
queue" and never drained.

Fix by checking pending.is_empty() directly instead of relying on the
epoch sentinel. This costs one extra mutex acquisition but ensures
correct behavior for retirements at epoch 0.

Also fix the test_ebr_retire_queue_multiple_epochs test which expected
drain_if_safe(2, 2) with oldest=0 to return empty. A gap of 2 (2-0=2)
is sufficient when min_epoch_gap is 2, so the drain should succeed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
